question_title: Will the interest in “onlyfarms” change between 2026-03-30 and 2026-04-07 according to Google Trends?
summary: The White House launched "OnlyFarms.gov" on Mar 27-28, creating a massive one-day spike to 100 due to the name's similarity to OnlyFans. The spike is already in steep exponential decay (100→64→37 in 2 days). The Mar 30 value in the resolution date range is 37; for "Decreases" the Apr 7 value just needs to be below 34. Exponential decay models project the Apr 7 value at 0-5, a decline of 30+ points. This is a shallow meme about a website name with no ongoing controversy or scheduled events to sustain interest. "Decreases" is near-certain.

factors.description: Classic viral meme decay pattern: 100→64→37 in 2 days, exponential decay projects to ~0-5 by Apr 7 — a 30+ point decline from the Mar 30 value of 37
One-time event (OnlyFarms.gov launch for National Agriculture Day Mar 27-28), no scheduled follow-ups or ongoing controversy to sustain interest
Shallow virality about a website NAME resembling OnlyFans — no substantive hook for sustained engagement beyond initial curiosity
Related queries show curiosity-phase searches ('what is onlyfarms', 'onlyfarms.gov') not sustained engagement patterns
Small possibility of renewed viral moment (comedian skit, political controversy, late-night TV coverage) temporarily boosting interest above 34
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement uncertainty (±2-3 points) is irrelevant given expected decline magnitude of 30+ points

reasoning:
The reflection passed. The analysis is clear-cut: this is a post-viral-spike decay question where all evidence points strongly to "Decreases."

**Key findings:**
- "OnlyFarms" spiked from 0 to 100 on Mar 28 when the White House launched OnlyFarms.gov for National Agriculture Day
- Already decaying rapidly: 100 → 64 → 37 over just 2 days
- In the resolution date range, the Mar 30 value is **37**
- Exponential decay projects Apr 7 value to **0-5** (well below the threshold of 34)
- This is a name-pun meme with no substantive hook for sustained interest and no scheduled follow-up events
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will the interest in “onlyfarms” change between 2026-03-30 and 2026-04-07 according to Google Trends?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42970)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=onlyfarms&date=2026-03-08%202026-04-07](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=onlyfarms&date=2026-03-08%202026-04-07) for 2026-04-07 is within 3 of the value at 2026-03-30. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "onlyfarms",
  "date": "2026-03-08 2026-04-07",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
